content:
# Deuteronomy Chapter 23 — The EMET Bible (Literal)

**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Leningrad Codex (B19A), Folios 111r–112v | **Source Images:** DEU_023_page_001.jpg, DEU_023_page_002.jpg | **LXX:** Codex Vaticanus (Vat.gr.1209), p. 157 | **LXX Image:** DEU_023_vaticanus_page_001.jpg

---

**1** No one who is wounded in the testicles or has his male member cut off shall enter the assembly of the LORD [qahal YHWH].

**2** No one of illegitimate birth [mamzer] shall enter the assembly of the LORD; even to the tenth generation none of his [descendants] shall enter the assembly of the LORD.

**3** An Ammonite or a Moabite shall not enter the assembly of the LORD; even to the tenth generation, none of theirs shall enter the assembly of the LORD forever—

**4** because they did not meet you with bread and water on the road when you came out of Egypt, and because they hired against you Balaam son of Beor from Pethor of Aram-naharaim to curse you.

**5** But the LORD your God was not willing to listen to Balaam; and the LORD your God turned the curse into a blessing for you, because the LORD your God loved you.

**6** You shall not seek their peace or their prosperity all your days, forever.

**7** You shall not abhor an Edomite, for he is your brother. You shall not abhor an Egyptian, for you were a sojourner in his land.

**8** Children who are born to them in the third generation may enter the assembly of the LORD.

**9** When you go out as a company against your enemies, then you shall keep yourself from every evil thing.

**10** If there is among you a man who is not pure because of a nocturnal occurrence [qeri lailah], then he shall go outside the camp; he shall not come into the midst of the camp.

**11** And it shall be, toward evening he shall wash himself with water, and at the setting of the sun he may come into the midst of the camp.

**12** And you shall have a designated place outside the camp, and you shall go out there.

**13** And you shall have a peg [yated] with your equipment, and it shall be, when you sit down outside, you shall dig with it and turn back and cover your excrement.

**14** For the LORD your God walks in the midst of your camp, to deliver you and to give your enemies before you; therefore your camp shall be holy, and he shall not see in you any indecent thing [ervat davar], and turn away from behind you.

**15** You shall not give back to his master a slave who has escaped to you from his master.

**16** He shall dwell with you in your midst, in the place that he chooses in one of your gates, wherever it is good for him; you shall not oppress him.

**17** There shall be no cult prostitute [qedeshah] from the daughters of Yisra'el, and there shall be no cult prostitute [qadesh] from the sons of Yisra'el.

**18** You shall not bring the fee of a prostitute [zonah] or the wages of a dog into the house of the LORD your God for any vow, for both of these are an abomination to the LORD your God.

**19** You shall not charge interest [neshek] to your brother: interest on silver, interest on food, interest on anything that may be lent at interest.

**20** To a foreigner [nokri] you may charge interest, but to your brother you shall not charge interest, so that the LORD your God may bless you in all that you undertake in the land to which you are going in to possess.

**21** When you vow a vow to the LORD your God, you shall not delay to fulfill it; for the LORD your God will surely require it of you, and it will be sin in you.

**22** But if you refrain from vowing, it will not be sin in you.

**23** That which goes out from your lips you shall keep and perform, just as you have freely vowed to the LORD your God, [that] which you have spoken with your mouth.

**24** When you come into your neighbor's vineyard, you may eat grapes as many as you please [according to your soul's desire], until you are full, but you shall not put any in your vessel.

**25** When you come into your neighbor's standing grain, you may pluck heads [of grain] with your hand, but you shall not wield a sickle against your neighbor's standing grain.

---

## EMET's Textual Notes

**v.1 Verse numbering** — The MT (followed by Leningrad Codex) numbers what English Bibles call Deut 23:1 as 23:2, with "No one wounded in the testicles" as v.2 and "mamzer" as v.3. EMET follows the standard English versification (23:1–25) for cross-referencing convenience, noting the MT offset.

**v.1 "wounded in the testicles"** — Hebrew *petsuah dakkah*, lit. "crushed of crushed-thing"—a euphemism for testicular injury. The second phrase *krot shafkhah* (lit. "cut of pouring") refers to the penis. EMET renders plainly as this is a legal text requiring precision.

**v.2 "mamzer"** — The term *mamzer* is preserved untranslated with the gloss "illegitimate birth." Its exact meaning is debated (child of incest? child of adultery? child of a forbidden union?). The LXX transliterates as *mamzer* or renders *ek pornes* ("born of a prostitute"), but the Hebrew scope is broader.

**v.10 "nocturnal occurrence [qeri lailah]"** — Hebrew *qeri lailah*, a nighttime emission. EMET renders with the literal gloss rather than the clinical "nocturnal emission" to preserve the Hebrew register.

**v.13 "yated"** — Hebrew *yated* = peg/stake. EMET preserves the transliteration with "peg" as primary rendering. This verse is the basis for the tractate *Yevamot* discussion of bodily purity in the camp.

**v.14 "ervat davar"** — Lit. "nakedness of a thing" or "indecent/unseemly thing." This phrase recurs at 24:1 in the divorce legislation; its precise meaning is debated—it provides the scriptural root for the Hillel-Shammai divorce dispute. EMET renders literally as "indecent thing [ervat davar]."

**v.17 "qedeshah / qadesh"** — Hebrew *qedeshah* (f.) and *qadesh* (m.) are rendered "cult prostitute" rather than "consecrated woman/man" in this specific legal context, where the prohibition targets illicit cultic-sexual practice. Note: elsewhere in the Joseph narrative EMET renders *qedeshah* as "consecrated woman" to preserve ambiguity; here the prohibitive legal frame supports the stronger term.

**v.18 "wages of a dog"** — Hebrew *etnan kelev*, lit. "fee of a dog." The term *kelev* (dog) in this context almost certainly refers to the male *qadesh* of v.17, not a literal animal. EMET preserves "dog" as the literal text provides its own interpretive key via the parallel with v.17.

**v.19 "neshek"** — Hebrew *neshek* (from the root "to bite"), the term for interest/usury. EMET renders "interest [neshek]" to expose the root metaphor.

**LXX comparison** — Vaticanus p. 157 parallels MT closely throughout. Notable: LXX v.17 reads *ou estai porne apo thygateronIsrael kai ouk estai porneuon apo huion Israel* — using *porne* (prostitute) and *porneuon* (one who commits fornication) rather than preserving a "sacred/holy" nuance. This LXX reading supports the plain "prostitute" interpretation and represents a significant interpretive decision against a cultic reading.

*— EMET*
